name: Sweet and Sour Salad Dressing
directions: Measure all ingredients into a jar with lid.
Shake to mix well.

similar.name: Old Fashioned Sour Cream Salad Dressing

name: Fat Free Creamy Ranch Dressing
directions: Into small bowl, measure all ingredients.
Mix with wire whisk or fork until blended.
Cover and refrigerate.
Stir before using.

similar.name: Buttermilk Herb Dressing (1 gram of fat)

name: French Dressing for the Lazy
directions: Put all the ingredients in a blender and pulse to blend in a smooth paste.
Season with salt and pepper.
Fill up in a clean bottle and refrigerate for 1 hour before using.

name: Honey Dijon Mustard Dressing
directions: Heat vinegar and honey, stirring until honey dissolves.
Cool.
Combine with remaining ingredients in shaker jar.
Shake until completely mixed.
Refrigerate.

name: Ranch Dressing
directions: SOUR CREME RECIPE Use 1 c.
lite sour creme in place of the buttermilk Mix mayo and spices.
Add buttermilk, a little at a time, mixing after each addition until smooth.
chill for several hours.

similar.name: Homemade Ranch Dressing and Dip Mix
Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper

name: White Wine Lemon Pepper Marinade / Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Can be kept in fridge for up to 2 weeks.
I use this as a marinade for beef, chicken, pork or veggies before grilling and as a basting sauce while grilling.

name: Thick and Creamy Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Place all except oil and mayonnaise in food processor or blender and process until smooth.
While processor is running add oil VERY SLOWLY and process until thick and creamy (about 4-5 minutes).
Pour into bowl and add 1/4 cup of mayonnaise and blend thoroughly with a fork.
Taste and adjust seasonings to your taste (sometimes I like to add a couple more tablespoons of Parmesan cheese).
Cover and chill until ready to use.

similar.name: The Best and Easiest Caesar Dressing Ever

name: Eggless Caesar Dressing
directions: Whisk vinegar,anchovy (or some salt) ,mustard, garlic& pepper together.
Whisk in olive oil slowly.
Whiskin lemon juice and Worcestershire (tobasco).

similar.name: Thick and Creamy Caesar Salad Dressing
The Best and Easiest Caesar Dressing Ever

name: Green Goddess Salad Dressing
directions: Stir all ingredients together until well blended.
Taste and add salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste.

name: Green Goddess Dressing ala Hellmanns
directions: Place first 9 ingredients in a blender; cover and blend until smooth.
Fold in yogurt.
Cover and chill until serving.
Good on a green salad or to dress cold seafood.

name: Italian Dressing - Low Sodium
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Blend on high until combined, approximately 30 seconds.
Pour into small pitcher or dressing cruet.
Cover and chill until ready to serve.
Shake or stir before pouring.
Lasts for several days in fridge.

name: Catalina French Dressing
directions: Cut ingredients in half if the quantity is too much for you.
Combine ingredients and blend thoroughly.
Store in cruets or empty salad dressing bottles and keep refrigerated.
Shake well before serving.

name: Olive Garden Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a blender until well mixed.
If this is a little to tart for your own personal tastes please add a little extra sugar.

name: Mustard Cream Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk cream and mustard until slightly thickened.
Add lemon juice, salt& pepper to taste (add a little at a time until you get it just right).
Let stand at room temperature for at least 15 minutes before serving.

name: Lemon-Yogurt Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk everything together except the yogurt.
When well blended whisk in the yogurt.

similar.name: American Kitchen Classic Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Tamarind Vinaigrette dressing
directions: Mix vinegar, mustard, salt and sugar.
Slowly add oil and continue to mix.

name: Mo's Red Salad Dressing
directions: In a large bowl, whisk the ketchup, sugar, salt and vinegar until the sugar is dissolved.
Whisk in the oil 1/2 c.
at a time.
MIx in onions.
Serve on your favorite salad veggies.
This makes a large quantity so refrigerate any unused portion.
When you are ready to use it again, you may need to let it set out at room temperature for about 10 minutes and then whisk again before serving.

name: No Oil Dressing
directions: Skake all ingredients in a tightly covered container.
Chill at least an hour.
Shake before serving.

name: Good Balsamic Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Follow the directions instructed on the back of the dry dressing mix package with the following adjustment: Instead of adding all regular vinegar to the "V" line on the cruet, add 1/2 cider vinegar and 1/2 balsamic vinegar.
Tastes great on greens!
I think it tastes it's best at room temp; so it would probably last that way for a few days; after then if it's not used, throw it in the fridge.

name: Great Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients well.
Chill.

name: Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Blend your mayonnaise, blue cheese and garlic.
Fold in the sour cream, then refrigerate 3-4 hours.
Keeps well.

name: Homemade Italian Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients except oil in a blender or food processor fitted with a steel blade.
Continue processing as you slowly pour in the oil.
Process until smooth.
Continue processing for 30 seconds or until the dressing is fully combined and thickened.
Keep in a covered container in the refrigerator until needed.
Remove from refrigerator 1/2 hour before serving and re-blend if necessary.

name: Mixed Herb Dressing
directions: Mix together the dry ingredients and store in an air-tight container.
Each time you need a dressing, shake together 1 tbsp.
of the dry herb mixed with 3/4 cup olive oil and 1/4 cup vinegar.

name: Mary's Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix together well.
Refridgerate.
Makes 4 cups dressing.

similar.name: Rich and Creamy Blue Cheese Dressing

name: Green Goddess Sauce
directions: In a blender container or food processor bowl combine yogurt, 1/4 cup sour cream and tarragon.
Cover and blend or process until smooth.
Transfer to a small bowl.
Stir in remaining sour cream, chives, lime juice and garlic.
Store any remaining sauce, covered, in the refrigerator up to 3 days and serve with salad greens.

similar.name: Old Fashioned Sour Cream Salad Dressing

name: Cucumber Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in a bowl and mix together with a fork or wire whisk.
Chill before serving.

name: World's Best Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Mix all the ingredients together.
Chill in refrigerator at least 2 hours before using.

similar.name: Chart House Blue Cheese Dressing - Copycat

name: Catalina Dressing
directions: Put everything in the container of your blender and whiz.
Or, let the kids put in a big jar and shake.
Chill at least 2 hours before using; you may have to re-shake.

name: Fat Free Asian Salad Dressing
directions: There are 2 options for preparing this dressing.
You can either whir ingredients in blender, which makes it a little frothy- which I like.
Or, you can finely mince the garlic and ginger and whip the rest of the ingredients together with a wire whisk.
Either way, it's fabulous!
You can easily double this recipe- just keep the 2 to 1 ratio for the garlic and ginger and the soy sauce and vinegar.

name: Creamy Basil Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender or food processor until smooth.
Chill until ready to serve.
This dressing will keep in the refrigerator for several days.

name: Onion Garlic Low Cal Salad Dressing
directions: Blend all the ingredients in a blender of food processor.
Chill before serving over salad.

name: Garlic Sauce X-Treme!
directions: Divide garlic into cloves and peel.
Blend all ingredients except oil in blender or food processor.
While blending, add oil slowly.
Chill for at least one hour.
Shake or wisk before serving.
Note: This is a very rich tasting garlic sauce which is great on a pasta (or anti-pasta) salad as well as seafoods or fresh veggies.
I have also used this once for a marinade for Steaks.

name: Avocado Dressing for Seafood Salads
directions: Blend eggs with lemon in a blender or food processor until it is lemon colored.
Slowly add the bubbling hot melted butter, just drip it in, using a slow speed on your blender.
Blend in the avocado and seasoning until smooth.
Chill 30 minutes.

similar.name: Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper

name: Thousand Island Dressing on the Fly
directions: Measure mayonnaise, chili sauce and pickle relish into a small bowl.
Stir together well.
If too thick, add a splash of milk.

name: Orange Vinaigrette
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Serve over salad.
Store in the refrigerator.

name: Creamy Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Mix well.
Add a little more sugar or vinegar, tasting till it's how you like it.

name: E-Z French Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in blender or food processor; blend until well mixed.

name: Salad Supreme Seasoning
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Store in a sealable container in the fridge.

name: Garlic And Lemon Vinaigrette
directions: Add the vinegar, garlic, lemon juice, zest, salt and pepper to a food processor bowl.
With the processor running, slowly drizzle in the oil.
Serve immediately or place in a glass container (with a lid) and refrigerate.

name: Ginger Dressing or Marinade
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a jar with a tight lid and shake well!

name: Basic Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Mix all the ingredients together, mix well.
This will keep well in the fridge for at least a week.

name: Easy French Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in blender.
Cover and mix on med until well mixed.
Place in covered jar and chill.

name: Dill Dressing
directions: Whisk the ingredients together.
Chill for 30 minutes.
Serve over crisp green salad of your choice.

similar.name: Onion Garlic Low Cal Salad Dressing

name: Lemonade Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients with electric mixer and chill.

name: Creamy Garlic Dressing
directions: Mix all together in jar with tight fitting lid.
Shake until mixed well.
Chill.

name: Italian Herb Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all in a jar with a tight fitting lid.
Shake well.
Shake again before serving.

name: Low Calorie Creamy Herb Dressing
directions: In a pyrex bowl mix the yogurt, vinegar, mustard& sugar.
If you want to make it ahead you can cover it and store in the fridge for 3 days.

similar.name: Buttermilk Herb Dressing (1 gram of fat)

name: mmm!!...Creamy Mayonnaise...mmm!!
directions: Place the mustard, salt, pepper, sugar, a FEW drops of the vinegar and the egg YOLK in a bowl and whisk together.
Continue whisking and at the same time, add the oil- but ONLY a LITTLE at a time.
The mixture should NOT curdle NOR become too thick- (insufficient whisking, poor quality ingredients or too hasty addition of the oil will cause curdling).
Now add the rest of the vinegar, and the lemon juice- a LITTLE at a time, to keep the mayonaise at the right consistency.
NOTE: if after refrigerating, you find the mayonaise a little too thick, it may be thinned down with a little amount of cream.

name: Brown Derby Old Fashioned French Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients, except oils.
Add olive oil and salad oil.
Mix well.
Chill and shake thoroughly before serving on salad.

similar.name: Brown Derby Old Fashioned French Dressing

name: Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: In mixer bowl, mix first 4 ingredients.
Add vinegar and beat on low for 4 minutes.
Add oil, beat another 10 minutes.
Add poppy seeds and beat another 5 minutes.

name: Honey-Mint Vinaigrette
directions: In a small bowl, whisk all ingredients together; you can also do this in a blender or food processor, but note that this will then puree the mint leaves.
Use right away, or refrigerate until needed; if refrigerated, whisk again to combine before using.
Use to dress a large salad; goes especially nice on a relatively plain salad (ie, just mixed greens and raw mushrooms is great).

name: Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Note that you can adjust the sugar and vinegar amounts to suit your taste, adding more or less of each as you prefer; also note that if you insist on using Miracle Whip, you'll likely want to cut the sugar back.
In a blender, food processor, or even in a bowl with a whisk, combine all ingredients well; taste, add more salt if you wish.
Either use right away to dress a coleslaw, or store, covered, in the refrigerator; keeps well for several days.

name: Buttermilk Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: Blend all in blender until smooth.
Flavor is better when it sets for a couple hours.
It's great when used for a dip.

similar.name: Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper

name: Bleu Cheese Mayonnaise
directions: In a food processor fitted with the metal blade, combine the egg, lemon juice, mustard, and vinegar.
Turn on the motor and process for 30 seconds.
With the motor running, slowly add the oil in a thin stream.
When the mayonnaise has emulsified, season with salt and pepper and add the blue cheese; pulse for 5 seconds.
Press through a fine-mesh sieve and store, covered, in the refrigerator until ready to use.

similar.name: Rich and Creamy Blue Cheese Dressing

name: Creamy Buttermilk Dressing
directions: Whisk together all ingredients.
Store refrigerated up to 4 days.
One serving= 1 tbsp.

similar.name: Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper
Buttermilk Herb Dressing (1 gram of fat)

name: Low-Fat Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and toss with salad greens.

similar.name: Thick and Creamy Caesar Salad Dressing
The Best and Easiest Caesar Dressing Ever

name: Chart House Blue Cheese Dressing - Copycat
directions: In a mixing bowl, combine first six ingredients and blend for 2 minutes on low speed.
Add the mayonnaise and blend for 30 seconds at low speed, then for 2 minutes at medium speed.
Slowly add half the blue cheese and blend at low speed for no more than four minutes.
Stir in remaining bleu cheese.
Cover and refrigerate 24 hours.

similar.name: Rich and Creamy Blue Cheese Dressing

name: Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper
directions: Whisk buttermilk, olive oil, lemon juice and mayonnaise in a small bowl until mixture is smooth and well blended.
Blend in remaining ingredients.
Cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Correct seasoning and allow to come to cool room temperature before serving.

similar.name: Buttermilk Herb Dressing (1 gram of fat)

name: Citrus Dressing
directions: In a blender, puree the orange juice, mustard, and olive oil.
Season to taste.
Serve over mixed fresh greens.

name: Herb Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: In container with a tight-fitting lid, combine vinegar, chicken stock, lemon juice and seasonings.
Shake well.
Add oil and shake again.
Refrigerate for up to two weeks.

name: Chicory Dressing
directions: Slice garlic in half, shake a pinch of salt in a wooden salad bowl, and rub the inside of the salad bowl with garlic halves.
Mix remaining ingredients in a small jar with lid.
Shake vigorously.
Spoon over mixed greens, allowing 1 tbsp.
per serving.

name: Tahini Yogurt Dressing
directions: Mix tahini and yogurt together. Stir in enough water so it has a nice consistency and then mix in parsley and tamari.

name: Parmesan Cheese Dressing
directions: Gradually blend milk into mayonnaise.
Mix in cheese, vinegar and worcestershire sauce.
Chill.
Serve on tossed salad greens or on a"meaty" chef salad.

name: Tangy Bacon Salad Dressing
directions: Add all ingredients to medium-sized bowl and blend until well mixed.
Store in refrigerator.
This dressing is good on fresh spinach, endive, or lettuce salads.

name: Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Mix ingredients in a blender until smooth.
Chill.
Spoon over crisp greens.

similar.name: Rich and Creamy Blue Cheese Dressing

name: Applebee's Oriental Dressing
directions: Mix em up.

similar.name: American Kitchen Classic Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Old Fashioned Sour Cream Salad Dressing
directions: Stir together salt, sugar, and paprika with lemon juice and vinegar until salt and sugar are dissolved.
Whisk in sour cream, blending until smooth.

name: Herbed Oil & Vinegar Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender or food processor.
Whirl until smooth.
Place in jar with tight-fitting lid.
Refrigerate for up to two weeks.
Shake well before serving.

name: Italian Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Shake vigorously.
Refrigerate in tightly sealed container.

name: Lime-Cilantro Dressing
directions: In a food processor fitted with a metal blade, combine the olive oil, lime juice, garlic, cilantro leaves, and black pepper.
Process until smooth.
Add sugar and salt to taste.

name: Garlic Mayo (Ali Oli)
directions: Put the egg, garlic and salt in a food processor.
Turn on, and whir until garlic is completely blended--I sometimes mash it first, even before adding it to the machine.
This is very important--Add the oil in a THIN steady stream--no thicker than spaghetti, especially at first.
Continue to add the oil in a steady stream until the mayo thickens.
Add the vinegar and process a couple seconds more.

Note:If for some reason the mayo does not thicken (it happens), remove the mixture from the food processor to a spouted container, put another egg into the food processor and add the previous mixture in a thin, steady stream, and it will thicken.

name: Simple Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Store in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before serving to allow the flavors to blend.

name: Italian Dressing Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together and force through a fine mesh sieve.
with back of a large spoon.
Store covered and use within 4-5 months.
For use: Combine 2 tablespoons of mix with 1/4 cup vinegar, 2/3cups oil, 2 tablespoons water.
Shake mixture vigorously.
*Use 1 envelope of the cream of chicken soup mix or 2 square soda crackers well crushed- plus 1 1/2 teaspoon chicken bouillon powder plus 1 teaspoon non dairy creamer powder.

name: Low-Fat Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Make it the night before if possible, minimum 1 hour to get it nice and tasty.

similar.name: Thick and Creamy Caesar Salad Dressing
The Best and Easiest Caesar Dressing Ever

name: Low Fat Caesar Dressing
directions: Put all the ingredient in your food processor and blend until well mixed (do it by hand with a whisk if you wish).
Chill before serving and use wiithin one day.

similar.name: Onion Garlic Low Cal Salad Dressing

name: Honey & Garlic Dressing
directions: Mix all the ingredients together with a wire whisk and allow to sit in the fridge overnight.
Pour the dressing over a salad composed of iceberg lettuce, red and green pepper strips, sliced radishes, cucumber and chopped parsley.

name: Orange Sesame Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk together all the ingredients.
chill and serve.

name: Buttermilk Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients in a bowl until smooth.
Serve over greens for salads, or use as a dip fpr fresh vegetables.

similar.name: Buttermilk Herb Dressing (1 gram of fat)
Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper

name: Blue Cheese Salad Dressing
directions: Stir well.
Serve at room temperature.

name: Creamy Parmesan Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and shake well.
Refrigerate.

similar.name: Buttermilk Herb Dressing (1 gram of fat)

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Combine and refrigerate in tightly sealed container.

name: Yogurt Dressing for sliced cucumbers
directions: Whisk well all ingredients and taste.
Chill well.
The yogurt and sour cream break down a bit to make a thin-ish sauce.

similar.name: Old Fashioned Sour Cream Salad Dressing

name: 1960's Catalina Dressing
directions: Add the ketchup, sugar, vinegar, onion, paprika and Worcestershire to a food processor bowl.
Pulse until blended.
With the processor running, slowly add the oil.
Check the seasonings.
Cover and refrigerate until needed.

name: My No-Fat Balsamic Dressing
directions: Combine the ingredients.
Adjust vinegar to taste, since it has a strong flavor.
Store in a covered container in the refrigerator.

name: Honey-Yogurt Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients, except yogurt in food processor.
Cover and process until smooth.
Place in a bowl and add yogurt, stirring well.
Cover and chill.

similar.name: Really Low Fat Greek Yogurt Dressing

name: Rosy Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients with a whisk until smooth.
Chill.
Serve over greens, or with veggie sticks.

name: Orange Ginger Dressing
directions: Put ginger and shallot in a food processor fitted with a metal blade; you could also just mince these with a handheld mincer.
Add remaining ingredients and blend.

name: Creamy Italian Dressing
directions: In jar with tight fitting lid; combine all ingredients.
Shake well.

name: Parmesan Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Cover and refrigerate any remaining dressing.

name: Oil-Free Italian Vinaigrette
directions: Combine water, powdered fruit pectin, parmesan cheese, white wine vinegar or vinegar, oregano, sugar, celery seed, dry mustard, pepper, paprika, garlic, and dash salt in a screw-top jar.
Cover and shake well (be sure all pectin is dissolved).
Chill several hours.
To store remaining dressing, cover and store in the refrigerator up to 3 days.
Shake before serving.

name: The Best and Easiest Caesar Dressing Ever
directions: Put all the ingredients into a large bowl and whisk together.
If the dressing is too thick, add a bit of water.
This will hold for weeks in your fridge.
This recipe is easily doubled or tripled.

similar.name: Thick and Creamy Caesar Salad Dressing

name: bleu cheese dressing
directions: whisk or blend all items till cheese is in small chunks.
let sit a short time before eating to let the flavors set.

name: Lemon-Garlic-Oregano Dressing
directions: Whisk together all of the ingredients, blending thoroughly.
Store refrigerated and shake well before using.

name: Fat Free Curried Yogurt Dip
directions: Mix all the ingredients together& serve.
MIx this dip just before serving.

name: Creamy Tofu Tarragon Dressing
directions: Put all but the tarragon in your blender or food processor, blend for 1 minute until everything is smooth Add tarragon whip 30 seconds.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour.

name: Basic Vinaigrette
directions: Combine the shallot, mustard, vinegar, salt, and pepper in a small mixing bowl and whisk until thoroughly combined.
Add the oil in a thin stream, whisking constantly.
If the sauce separates before being used it me be recombined by whisking vigorously for a few seconds.

name: My Own Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Combine mayonnaise, lemon juice, celery seed, sugar and salt and pepper.
For cole slaw, toss dressing with shredded cabbage and carrot.
Chill.

name: Creamy Ranch Dressing
directions: Place all the ingredients in the bowl of a food processor fitted with the steel blade.
Process until smooth.

similar.name: Buttermilk Herb Dressing (1 gram of fat)

name: Calorie Watchers Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients well.
Chill.

name: Russian Salad Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients EXCEPT oil in blender.
Cover and process for 30 seconds.
Remove feeder cap; slowly add oil while processing on med.
Process until smooth.
Chill.

name: Sweet Yummy Salad Dressing
directions: Mix well.
That's it.

similar.name: Really Low Fat Greek Yogurt Dressing

name: Cinnamon Honey Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in blender or food processor and mix well.
Store in refrigerator.

name: No-Oil Salad Dressing #2
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Keep Refrigerated.

name: Horseradish Cream Dressing
directions: Beat cream until it begins to thicken, add vinegar gradually; continue beating until cream is stiff; add salt and pepper and fold in the horseradish.

similar.name: Old Fashioned Sour Cream Salad Dressing

name: Brown Derby Old Fashioned French Dressing
directions: Blend together all ingredients except oils.
Add olive oil and salad oil and mix well again.
Chill.
Shake before serving.

similar.name: Brown Derby Old Fashioned French Dressing

name: Creamy Tarragon Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, whisk all ingredients together until blended, or combine in a blender until smooth.

name: Allegedly Original Cobb Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk egg yolks vigorously until they turn pale yellow.
Slowly incorporate oil to form an emulsion.
Add remaining ingredients and season with salt and pepper.

similar.name: Brown Derby Old Fashioned French Dressing

name: French Dressing II
directions: Place all the ingredients in a covered jar and shake well.
Or, combine the salt, vinegar and pepper, stir well to dissolve the salt, then gradually beat in the oil.

name: Roquefort Dressing
directions: Mash cheese with a little oil.
Add worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, vinegar and balance of oil.
Shake well until creamy.

name: Dill Cream Dressing
directions: Beat together all ingredients and season well with salt and pepper.
More mustard and some lemon juice can be added to suit individual taste.
Milk or cream can be added for a thinner dressing.
Chill an hour or more before serving.

name: Basil-Sesame Dressing
directions: Whisk together all ingredients.
Add pepper.
Refrigerate till ready to use.

name: Dijon Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: Drop garlic into food processor, using the chopping blade.
Add all remaining ingredients and process until smooth.
Refrigerate till ready to use.

name: Greek Salad Dressing
directions: Simply pour all ingredients into a container (bottle is best).
Shake vigorously until well mixed.
Store tightly at room temperature.

similar.name: Really Low Fat Greek Yogurt Dressing

name: Italian Herb Dressing
directions: Combine ingredients in jar.
Cover and shake well.
Chill to blend flavors.
Remove garlic.
Shake again before serving.

name: Homemade Ranch Dressing and Dip Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients and place in air tight jar.
Stir one teaspoon of dressing/dip mix into 8 ounces of sour cream at a time to taste.
Pour buttermilk into sour cream mixture until it is the desired thickness for dressing.
Omit buttermilk for dip or use very little.
This may also be added to a mayonnaise and buttermilk mixture if preferred or added to "soy" sour cream substitute with soy milk and a teaspoon of vinegar to make it vegan.

name: Parmesan Pepper Dressing
directions: In your blender or food processor combine buttermilk, cottage cheese, Parmesan, vinegar & garlic.  Process until smooth.
Stir in the pepper.

name: Italian Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bottle.
Shake to blend.
Allow flavors to blend about an hour or so,  Will even be more flavorful the following day. Be sure to refrigerate any leftovers.
Makes about 1 cup.

name: White Wine Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients in a container.
To serve, whisk thoroughly and pour desired amount over salad.
Refrigerate unused portions, but you may bring them up to temperature before serving again.

name: Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients except oil and poppy seeds, mix well.
Slowly add oil and poppy seeds and mix.
Refridgerate for a couple hours.

name: Southwestern Style Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker jar or bowl.
Shake well or mix well.
Chill for an hour.
Shake again before serving.
Serve on your favorite greens.

name: Lone Star's Bleu Cheese Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together by hand in a small bowl until smooth.
Cover and chill for 30 minutes before serving.
Makes 1 cup.

name: Buttermilk Herb Dressing (1 gram of fat)
directions: Mix all ingredients together well.
Cover and chill.

similar.name: Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper

name: Zesty Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Mix ingredients together well.
Cover and chill.

name: Creamy Cucumber Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Cover and chill.

name: Creamy Celery Seed Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small mixing bowl and stir well.
Cover and chill before serving.

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing bowl and mix well.
Cover and chill before serving.

name: Easy Creamy Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix with mixer.
Pour over cabbage just befor serving.

name: The Best Coleslaw Dressing--No Kidding!
directions: MIX TOGETHER-- WHEW, THAT WAS HARD!

name: Ranch Dressing and Seasoning Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Blend at high speed until smooth.
If you want to use this to make salad dressing combine 1 tablespoon mix with 1 cup mayonnaise and 1 cup milk.
Otherwise use 1 tablespoon in any recipe calling for an envelope of ranch dressing mix.

similar.name: Homemade Ranch Dressing and Dip Mix

name: Egg Dressing With Tarragon & Gherkins
directions: In a bowl combine the whole egg and yolk, mustard, and the vinegar.
Mix to combine.
Add the oil in a stream, whisking while adding until the dressing is combined.
Add the gherkins, tarragon, and salt and pepper to taste and mix well.
The dressing can be kept covered and chilled, for several days.
Serve the dressing with cold meat, poultry, or vegetables.

similar.name: Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper

name: Quick and Easy Salad Dressing
directions: Stir all ingredients together.
Add milk a little at a time until mixture is as thin as regular salad dressing.
Pour over green salad JUST BEFROE serving.

name: Honey Mustard Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk together the vinegar and mustard and then drizzle in the honey and then the oil.
Mix until well blended.
Add salt to taste.
Will last 2 weeks if stored covered and refrigerated.

name: Oriental Dressing
directions: Combine lemon juice, apple juice, ginger, Nama Shoyu, dates, water, and garlic in food processor, and process well.
With the processor running, pour sesame and olive oil in a thin stream until well blended.
Add flax seed and blend well.
Serve over spinach or other greens and sprinkle with sesame seeds.

name: Fat-Free Italian-Balsamic Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients except Parmesan and whisk well.
Pour over salad,toss to coat, and top with grated Parmesan cheese.

name: Mama's Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Mix everything together and pour over your cabbage mixture.

name: Parmesan Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Mince garlic and mash to a paste with 1/4 teaspoon salt, then whisk together with vinegar, lemon juice, basil, parmesan, remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt, and pepper.
Add oil in a slow stream, whisking until well blended.
Makes about 2/3 cup.

name: Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, combine the first six ingredients.
Slowly whisk in oil.
Cover and refrigerate.
Serve with fruit or salad.

name: Sesame Soy Vinaigrette
directions: Process all ingredients EXCEPT OIL in a blender or food processor until smooth.
With machine running, slowly pour in oil until mixture is thickened and blended.
Pour into bottles or jars.
Cover tightly.
Shake before using.

name: Creamy Garlic Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender; blen until smooth.

similar.name: Buttermilk Herb Dressing (1 gram of fat)

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk together vinegar, sugar, mustard, salt and pepper.
Slowly add oil; stir in chives.

similar.name: Choose Your Own Flavor Herb Vinaigrette

name: French Dressing
directions: Mix everything together in the blender and let it rip.
Refrigerate for up to 1 week.

name: Asian Shredded Chicken Coleslaw
directions: In a large bowl, combine the first 6 ingredients.
Add chicken and let stand 5 minutes.
Add cabbage, celery, onion, and cilantro.
Season.
Chill at least 1 hour to blend flavors.
Toss now and then.

similar.name: Chicken Salad With Chipotle Cilantro Dressing
Onion Garlic Low Cal Salad Dressing

name: Creamy Honey Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together, mixing till well combined.

name: Rich and Creamy Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Mash blue cheese and buttermilk in a small bowl with fork until mixture resembles cottage cheese with small curds.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
ADD Salt and Pepper to taste.
Can be refrigerated up to 14 days.

name: Creamy Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk balsamic vinegar, chives, parsley, mayonnaise, Dijon mustard and sugar together.
Add olive oil while whisking until all the oil is incorporated.
Season with salt and fresh ground black pepper.
Refrigerate until needed.

name: Nut Tree Clear French Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients, beating well.
Store in covered jar in refrigerator.
Shake or beat well before serving.
If a milder dressing is desired, add 1/4 to 1/3 more salad oil and mix well.

name: Dog Team Salad Dressing
directions: Mash garlic and rub well into dry ingredients.
Add vinegar and beat in oil.

name: Blue Cheese Dressing by Bob
directions: Mix all of the ingredients together and get out the greens.

name: Tarragon Salad Dressing
directions: In a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine the first nine ingredients; shake well.
Serve over salad greens.

name: tofu salad dressing
directions: add all ingredients to a blender, liquify, chill until ready to serve.

name: All-Purpose Salad Dressing
directions: In a small bowl whisk together wine, vinegar, and mustard until blended.
Slowly pour in olive oil, whisking constantly.
Whisk in oregano, parsley, and garlic.
Use for a dressing on mixed greens.

name: Dijon Vinaigrette
directions: Mix.
Store in refrigerator.

name: Dill Dressing
directions: Mix the mustard with the vinegar in a bowl.
Whisk in the olive oil, then stir in the dill, chives, and tarragon.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Add a little more vinegar or oil if you like.

name: Garlic & Herb Salad Dressing
directions: Shake all ingredients in a small jar with a tight fitting lid.
Let stand at room temperature for at least 1 hour.
The longer the better.
Shake again and use to dress any crisp green salad.
Store in jar and refrigerate 4 to 5 days.
Always shake before using.

name: Ginger Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients into blender or food processor and blend until vegetables are pureed.
Store in covered jar.
Shake thoroughly before using.

name: Moroccan Salad Dressing
directions: Mix together all the ingredients and let sit for a while to allow the flavours to blend.

name: Peanut Butter Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk together first 7 ingredients in a bowl until blended and smooth.
Serve over mixed salad greens.

name: Five-vinegar Dill Dressing
directions: Combine vinegars in a measuring cup.
Finely mince the dill in parsley in a food processor; add mustard, honey and vinegars.
With the motor running, slowly drizzle in the olive oil and continue to process for 1 minute.
Serve chilled or at room temperature, stirring or shaking before using.
Store in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.

name: Low-fat Creamy Cheese Salad Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, combine the mayonnaise, sour cream and cheese.
Refrigerate in a covered container for at least an hour.
The dressing will keep for about 1 week in the refrigerator.

name: Creamy Black Peppercorn Dressing
directions: Combine yogurt, buttermilk and mayonnaise in a large jar; add parsley, chives, black pepper, basil, tarragon, thyme, salt and paprika, shake well.
Refrigerate at least 30 minutes before using.
Taste and adjust seasonings.

similar.name: Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper

name: Ginger Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Refrigerate until ready to use.

name: No-Raw-Egg Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: You can prepare this dressing by hand with a whisk and some elbow grease, but it's easier to use a blender or food processor.
Put all ingredients except oils and parmesan in bowl of food processor or blender (or in a bowl) and combine well.
With food processor (or blender) running, slowly dribble in oils--or slowly whisk in oils, if making by hand.
You could use either all olive oil or all canola oil, but I prefer to use both.
Lastly, blend in the parmesan.
Taste; adjust seasonings as you wish (you may want to add salt, but the anchovy paste and worcestershire do provide the recipe with a fair amount of saltiness).

Either use right away, or refrigerate; it thickens when chilled, however, so you may have to give it a little whisk to loosen it up if you've stashed it in the fridge (it makes a great dip when thickened, though).
This dressing makes enough to dress one large caesar salad.

name: Raspberry Balsamic -Walnut Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk together all ingredients until blended.
Refrigerate.

name: Western Salad Dressing
directions: Shake all well in a jar.
Do not refrigerate.
Good on any salad.

name: Easy, Spicy, 1000 Island Dressing
directions: Combine ingredients.
serve on salad or burgers.

similar.name: Thousand Island Dressing on the Fly

name: Gorgonzola Salad Dressing / Dip
directions: Mix all ingredients together with hand mixer.
Refrigerate to blend flavors.
Use as dip or salad dressing.

name: Weyback Honey Mustard Salad Dressing
directions: Whirl all ingredients in blender until smooth, scraping down sides as necessary.
Refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Basil Buttermilk Salad Dressing
directions: Mince garlic and mash to a paste with the salt (I used a mortar and pestle).
Whisk together buttermilk, mayo, basil and garlic paste in a small bowl; chill, covered, 1 hour to allow flavors to develop.

similar.name: Buttermilk Herb Dressing (1 gram of fat)
Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper

name: N Z's Best Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients into a bowl and beat well.
I beat mine using my electric mixer and large bowl.
I use 'as is' but can be thinned just prior to use by adding a little milk or cream to get the consistency you like.

name: Asian Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: Mix first 5 ingredients in a bowl or food processor.
If using a bowl: SLOWLY drizzle in the sesame and peanut oil, whisking constantly so that the dressing will emulsify.
If using a food processor, leave it running while you drizzle in the oil.
When dressing is well combined, add sesame seeds and scallions.
Serve immediately or refrigerate and use within a week.

name: Spicy French Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients except oil in blender.
Blend until smooth.
With blender running, add oil in a slow steady stream.
Continue blending until mixture is thick.
Chill several hours before serving.

name: Wasabi Salad Dressing
directions: Mix soy sauce, vinegar, and sugar in a bowl until sugar is dissolved.
Add sesame oil and wasabi paste; whisk well.
Chill before using.

name: Parmesan-pepper Dressing
directions: Put the lemon juice, Parmesan, garlic, milk, and worcestershire sauce in a food processor, and pulse until the mixture is fairly smooth.
With the machine running, add the oil slowly until the dressing is emulsified.
Add the salt, pepper, oregano, and parsley, and pulse twice to blend.

name: Greek Vinaigrette
directions: Combine and whisk ingredients together.
If desired, chill before using.

name: Low-Fat Japanese Miso Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients with a small whisk.

name: Blue Cheese Buttermilk Creamy Dressing
directions: In a food processor or heavy-duty blender, combine buttermilk, sour cream, garlic, sugar, paprika, and salt. Blend until smooth. Add blue cheese, and pulse quickly once or twice. Do not blend. You want small chunks of blue cheese.
Refrigerate 4 hours or more before serving to let flavors blend.

similar.name: Rich and Creamy Blue Cheese Dressing

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette With Fresh Herbs
directions: Combine vinegar, herbs, garlic, salt and pepper in small mixing bowl.  Mix well with a whisk.  Slowly pour in the olive oil while continually whisking in order to form an emulsion.
Refrigerate and use within a week.

name: Ginger Salad Dressing
directions: Combine in jar with tight-fitting lid. Shake well.

name: "hibachi"-Style Salad Dressing
directions: To make ginger juice, peel some fresh ginger and cut into small pieces. Put pieces in a garlic press and squeeze.
Combine all ingredients except mayonnaise in a small bowl and whisk until honey is dissolved.
Add mayonnaise and whisk again until smooth.
Good on green salads, cucumbers, and just about anything else!

name: Dijon Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients and shake well.

name: Creamy Pepper-Garlic Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients until well blended.  Store refrigerated in an airtight container for up to 1 month.
Variations: for Creamy Caesar whisk 1/2 cup Creamy Pepper-Garlic Dressing with 2 T. grated Parmesan cheese and 1 teaspoons Worcestershire until blended.
For Thousand Island, whisk 1/2 cup CPGD with 3 T. ketchup or chili sauce and 4 teaspoons dill or sweet relish.

similar.name: Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper

name: Honey Dressing
directions: Mix dry ingredients.
Add honey,vinegar, lemon juice and onion.
Pour oil into mixture in a very slow stream while beating constantly with beater.
Toss on a fresh fruit salad and serve on a bed of fresh mixed greens.

name: Nana's Special Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Mix together the cream and Miracle Whip.
Add vinegar,, sugar, salt and pepper, whipping with a whisk until totally blended.
Pour over 1/2 head cabbage and refrigerate for several hours, stirring occasionally.

name: Low Fat Tahini Dressing
directions: Cut garlic and ginger into smaller pieces.
Put all the ingredients in the food processor and process until the dressing becomes creamy.
Adjust the amount of ingredients to your liking.

name: Good Gracious--A Gallon of Greek Dressing
directions: In a very large container, mix together the olive oil, garlic powder, oregano, basil, pepper, salt, onion powder, and Honey Dijon-style mustard.
Pour in the vinegar, and mix vigorously until well blended.
Store tightly covered in the refrigerator.
Allow flavors to meld overnight for the best flavor.

name: Creamy Cumin Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar and shake till well combined.
Adjust water and vinegar for creaminess(see below). Store in the fridge.
Note: This works best if you have a very smooth, very creamy tahini. Personally I swear by the cheapo stuff from Bulk Barn.

name: Clemson Blue Cheese Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Store in covered container in refrigerator.

name: Mustard Chive Vinaigrette
directions: Using a whisk or fork, in a small bowl combine all ingredients except the oil. Slowly add the oil, whisking vigorously, until the vinaigrette is emulsified.
Pour over your favorite salad greens and toss.
Store remaining vinaigrette in the refrigerator, in a tightly sealed glass jar, for up to one week.
To warm cold vinaigrette, place jar in a small bowl of hot tap water for a few minutes.

name: El Torito Cilantro and Pepita Salad Dressing
directions: Place all dressing ingredients except cilantro, mayonnaise and water in a blender of food processor.
Blend approximately 10 seconds, and then add cilantro little by little until blended smooth.
Depending on size of blender, it may be necessary to do in batches.
Place mayonnaise and water in a large stainless steel bowl, and mix with a wire whip until smooth.
Add the blended ingredients to the mayonnaise mixture, and mix thoroughly.
Place in an airtight container and refrigerate.
Will keep for three days.

name: Roquefort Cream Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients into a blender in the order given.
Blend for 30 seconds.  Note: for thicker dressing, blend only half the cheese and mash the rest with a fork to add later.
Keep refrigerated.  Dressing solidifies as it chills but readily softens at room temperature.

name: Choose Your Own Flavor Herb Vinaigrette
directions: In a quart jar, combine the oils, vinegar and mustard.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, and add the garlic clove.
Cover and refrigerate.
Before using, shake well and pour about a cup of dressing into a smaller jar.
Add the mix-ins of your choice, cover and shake. Makes 4 cups.

name: Creamy Parmesan Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a blender until creamy and smooth.
Pour into a bowl, cover and store in the refrigerator. Makes about 2 cups.

similar.name: Choose Your Own Flavor Herb Vinaigrette

name: Orange Curry Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk all ingredients together.

name: Greek Salad Dressing
directions: Blend together and enjoy.

similar.name: Really Low Fat Greek Yogurt Dressing

name: Orange Scallion Vinaigrette
directions: Place all the ingredients except the oils in a food processor and process until combines.
With the machine running, drizzle the oils slowly through pusher tube and process until emulsified.
Taste for seasoning; you may need to add a little more honey or soy sauce.

name: Chilli and Lime Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients into a shaker or small screw top jar and shake until blended.
Toss through green salad leaves.

name: Craig Claiborne's Vinaigrette
directions: In a bowl, add mustard, salt, pepper and vinegar.
Stir well; to make smooth, whisk while adding the oil slowly.
You want to get it thick like mayonnaise.
Add the shallots and mix well.

similar.name: Choose Your Own Flavor Herb Vinaigrette

name: Herb Salad With Chive Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk all ingredients(except salad mix)together in a small bowl until blended.
Toss salad mix with Chive vinaigrette to coat.

similar.name: Choose Your Own Flavor Herb Vinaigrette

name: Ww 1 Point - Peppercorn Parmesan Dressing
directions: Combine the vinegar and oil in a small jar with a lid and shake.
Add the cheese and pepper, seal the jar, and shake again.
Refridgerate the dressing in airtight container for up to 1 week.

name: Maple Vinaigrette
directions: combine the cider vinegar, maple syrup, Dijon mustard, parsley, salt and pepper. Whisk to combine and then whisk in the oil.
Set aside until ready to use.
Whisk the vinaigrette before pouring some over each salad.

name: Sesame Miso Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients in jar or dressing bottle. Cap and shake well.

name: Citrus Vinaigrette
directions: combine all ingredients together and whisk until well incorporated.
Drizzle 2-3 tsp of dressing over your salads.

Note: this is a great dressing to go with any green salad that is combined with some sort of fruit (pear, orange segments, grapes, strawberries, dried sweet cranberries), add some type of nut (almonds, walnuts), and cheese (crumbled bleu, feta, gorgonzola).  MAKE IT YOUR OWN!

name: Spring Salad With a Really Cool Dressing!
directions: Layer salad in a beautiful spring  bowl as listed.
Blend salad dressing ingredient's  in a blender.
Pour over salad.
Enjoy!

name: Coconut Curry Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in a jar.
Seal and shake vigarously.
Serve with a salad, stir fry, or anything else you can think of!

name: Curry Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients and whisk until fairly smooth and well combined. Transfer to an airtight jar and refrigerate for up to 2 weeks.  Use chilled or at room temperature.

name: Mexican Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender or food processor and puree until smooth.
If not serving immediately, leave the dressing in the blender or processor until ready to serve, then pulse it a bit to re-combine and transfer to a serving container.

name: Creamy Pepper Dressing
directions: Mix mayonnaise and sour cream together till smooth.
Stir in remaining ingredients.

name: Lorri's Famous Fresh Salsa
directions: Put 1 can of tomatoes in the Food Processor/Blender with chopping blade.
Pulsate/Blend on lowest speed for about 45 seconds.
Add the chilies, jalapeños, onion, cilantro, garlic, salt, chili powder, lime juice and the 2nd. can of stewed tomatoes.
Pulsate/blend again for 30 seconds.
Fold in the chopped fresh Roma tomatoes.
Best when chilled for a few hours. It allows all the different flavors to blend.
Serve with your favorite Mexican dish or tortilla chips.

similar.name: Chicken Salad With Chipotle Cilantro Dressing

name: Gretchen's Salad Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, whisk vinegar and mustard.
Whisk in remaining ingredients.

name: Creamy Buttermilk Dressing
directions: Blend (in blender) until smooth. Chill at least 1 hour before serving.

similar.name: Buttermilk Dressing with Herbs & Green Pepper
Buttermilk Herb Dressing (1 gram of fat)

name: Sweet Salad Dressing
directions: Mix ingredients until sugar dissolves completely.
Serve on salad fixings of your choice!

name: Amazing Pickle Juice Ranch Dressing
directions: Combine mayonnaise, sour cream and dill pickle juice in a medium sized bowl.
Add in buttermilk.
Stir in the rest of the ingredients.
Chill for about 30 minutes.
Enjoy.

similar.name: Homemade Ranch Dressing and Dip Mix

name: Caesar Salad Mix
directions: Spread the lemon rind on a paper towel and microwave on High for 4 to 4 1/2 minutes or until it's dry, stirring several times (alternatively, you can dry it at room temperature for 1 day).
Combine all ingredients in an airtight container.
May be stored for up to one month in the refrigerator.

similar.name: American Kitchen Classic Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Oriental Coleslaw
directions: Chop the entire bunch of green onions.
Crush both packages of ramen noodles, setting aside the flavoring packets. This is easiest to do before opening the noodles, just pound it with your hand. Then open it up and crush any larger pieces.
Combine the first 5 ingredients in a large bowl.
In a small bowl, combine sugar and both flavor packets from the ramen noodles.
Add the apple cider vinegar and soy sauce, stirring well.
Add the vegetable oil. Stir again, until sugar is dissolved.  (I usually put the dressing in a sealable container, and shake it up.).
Add the dressing to the salad mix, tossing until evenly distributed.
Enjoy!

name: Chicken Salad With Chipotle Cilantro Dressing
directions: Mix chicken, onion, and peppers in a salad bowl.
Place all dressing ingredient in food processer and process until blended.
Pour dressing over chicken and stir to blend.
Chill for at least 30 minutes before serving.

This salad tastes even better the next day, so it can be made 1-2 days in advance.  You can adjust the heat by adding more or less chipotle peppers or you can adjust the sweetness by adding more or less honey.

similar.name: Onion Garlic Low Cal Salad Dressing

name: Really Low Fat Greek Yogurt Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together, and pour over an entree-sized salad.

name: Elderflower Vinaigrette
directions: Place all the ingredients in a screw top jar, screw on the lid and shake vigorously until the all the ingredients are incorporated and creamy.
Serve immediately on baby leaf salads or as a dressing on hot new potatoes. The dressing also makes a good light sauce for fish or baked chicken.

similar.name: Choose Your Own Flavor Herb Vinaigrette

name: Basil 'honey' Mustard Dressing (Raw Vegan)
directions: Put all ingredients in the blender and go to town.
This will keep for up to a week in the refrigerator.

name: Swedish Creamy Dill Prawn Toasts With Caviar - Skagenrora
directions: Carefully remove the shells and heads from the prawns. Put the sour cream, mayonnaise, chopped dill, chives and lemon juice in a large bowl. Season with salt and pepper, then stir in the prawns. Add more lemon juice if needed.
Melt the butter in a large frying pan. Working in batches, add the rye bread and fry until golden brown on both sides.
Serve the prawn mixture piled on top of the fried bread and garnish each portion with a dab of caviar or lumpfish roe and a frond of dill.

name: Vegan French Dressing No Mayo
directions: Pour the ingredients into a blender and blend on high for 30 seconds, pour into a sterilized bottle and chill in the fridge for at least 2 hours before eating. Yields about 2 1/2 cups.

name: Low-Fat Creamy Caesar Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients into a blender and puree until smooth.  Cover and chill until ready to serve.

name: Horseradish Vinaigrette (Tahiti)
directions: Combine horseradish, garlic, mustard, sambal, honey, soy sauce, and lime juice in a bowl.
Stirring constantly, slowly drizzle in oil until blended.
Add salt to taste.

name: Rosemary Vinaigrette (Vinagreta Con Romero)
directions: Cover rosemary with hot water and soak about 3 minutes.  Drain.  This step is optional.
Place rosemary in a jar with a lid.
Add remaining ingredients.
Shake well.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Strain before using.

name: Mexican-Style Italian Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar with a lid.
Cover and shake well.
Refrigerate at least 4 hours, or overnight.
Strain before using (optional).

name: Mexican-Style Italian Dressing With Oregano
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Strain before serving (optional).

name: French Dressing (1908)
directions: Combine salt and paprika (or pepper) in a shallow dish.
Add mustard, if using.
Pour in the oil and mix well.
Beat in vinegar a little at a time, beating constantly.
Serve immediately.

similar.name: Brown Derby Old Fashioned French Dressing

name: Green Goddess Salad Dressing (1953)
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.

similar.name: Thick and Creamy Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Best Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Spread lemon rind on paper towel. Microwave on HIGH for 4 to 4-1/2 minutes or until dry, stirring several times, or dry for 1 day at room temperature.
Combine with cheese, oregano, mustard, pepper, garlic powder and cayenne.
Refrigerate in airtight container for up to 1 month. Makes 3/4 cup, enough for 3 salads. Whisk together ingredients shown above for one salad, 12 cups romaine lettuce.

similar.name: Thick and Creamy Caesar Salad Dressing
American Kitchen Classic Caesar Salad Dressing

name: American Kitchen Classic Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Mash the anchovy with the garlic and salt until it is a paste. Whisk in the mayonnaise, vinegar, lemon juice mustard and worcestershire sauce and combine well. Whisk in the olive oil and then the parmesan cheese. Chill. Whisk again just before mixing with the salad leaves then top with the croutons.

similar.name: Thick and Creamy Caesar Salad Dressing

name: American Kitchen Classic French Vinaigrette
directions: In a bowl, whisk together the vinegar, mustard, shallots and 1/4 tsp salt. Combine well.

Still whisking the mixture, slowly drizzle in the olive oil. When the olive oil has been fully integrated, add the black pepper and give it another quick beating. Chill and remix if it has separated before serving.

similar.name: Onion Garlic Low Cal Salad Dressing

name: American Kitchen Classic Italian Vinaigrette
directions: In a bowl, whisk together the vinegar, mustard, shallots and 1/4 tsp salt. Combine well.

Still whisking the mixture, slowly drizzle in the olive oil. When the olive oil has been fully integrated, add the black pepper and give it another quick beating. Chill and remix if it has separated before serving.

similar.name: Onion Garlic Low Cal Salad Dressing

name: Classic Vinaigrette
directions: Dissolve the salt and pepper in the vinegar and whisk in the Dijon mustard.
Gradually incorporate the oil in a steady stream, whisking continuously until thick.
Mix in the chopped garlic, chervil and shallots.

similar.name: American Kitchen Classic Caesar Salad Dressing
